LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, FEB-26-2018 HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION OF VANESSA ARGABRIGHT IS HELD. THE CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS GRANTED IN PART. BASED ON THE CURRENT RECORD, THE COURT IS INCLINED TO ALLOW PLAINTIFF TO RECOVER $250/PAY PERIOD ($500/MONTH) TO SERVICE THE DEBT. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. (D302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Feb-26-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION OF VANESSA ARGABRIGHT.

The claim of exemption is GRANTED in part. Based on the current record, the court is inclined to allow plaintiff to recover $250/pay period ($500/month) to service the debt.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/RBU)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Jan-22-2018 Continued To Feb-26-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Vivian Leung Lerche of the law offices of Vivian Leung Lerche (415-680-5731) specially appears as counsel for judgment creditor/opposing party

Vanesa Rivers (aka Argabright) (408-472-1091) appears as self-represented judgment debtor/exemption claimant

The cause is argued by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. It is determined that claimant/judgment debtor requires time to appraise value of business tools and ascertain installment payment amount.

HEARING RE VANESSA RIVERS (AKA ARGOBRIGHT), JUDGMENT DEBOTOR'S CLAIM OF EXEMTION is CONTINUED to February 26, 2018 at 9:30 AM in Department 302.

Judge: Newton Lam: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, e-mail: mtorreano@sftc.org (L&M302)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-26-2012 Off Calendar
